name: Abdullah ibn Aqeel
title: Devoted Nephew
shortDescription: The nephew of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who, along with the other sons of Aqeel, fought heroically and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Aqeel is the son of Aqeel ibn Abi Talib and the nephew of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). During the event of Karbala, the family of Aqeel was one of the most loyal families to the Master of Martyrs (AS), with many sons and grandsons of Aqeel attaining martyrdom alongside the Imam.

After the martyrdom of Muslim ibn Aqeel in Kufa, Abdullah and his brothers accompanied Imam Hussain (AS) with an even firmer resolve to avenge their brother's blood and defend the sanctuary of Wilayah.

On the day of Ashura, when the Imam's companions were martyred one after another, it was the turn of Aqeel's family. Abdullah entered the battlefield with a warrior's spirit and, while reciting epic poetry, engaged in battle with the Kufans.

With great skill, he killed a number of enemies. Uthman ibn Khalid al-Juhani and Bishr ibn Khawt, who were full of malice, attacked him and martyred him. Imam Sajjad (AS) later prayed for the family of Aqeel, saying: 'May God reward you with goodness.'
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَقيلِ بْنِ أَبِي طالِبٍ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Abdullah, the son of Aqeel ibn Abi Talib.
